summary: Scorching Scarlet > Fire, deep blues, and a fiery second‑set jam
blurb: The Madison show delivers a monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence, a ripping Deep Elem Blues/Bertha combo, and an inspired second set that rockets through Space, Spanish Jam and Truckin'. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while Brent’s keyboards add vivid color. The encore of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue caps a night of high‑energy playing.
review: On December 3, 1981 at the Dane County Coliseum in Madison, the Dead turned in one of their most exhilarating late‑'80 performances. After a shaky start with a flubbed Jack Straw, the band quickly found its groove, launching into a deep‑blues‑laden Deep Elem Blues that segued into an electrifying Bertha, showcasing Jerry’s “exquisite” leads and Brent’s soaring keyboards. The second set is the show’s crown jewel: a monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue that many reviewers called “monster” and “high‑energy,” followed by a tight Estimated Prophet, a soulful He’s Gone, then a seamless drum‑to‑Space transition into a Spanish‑style jam that erupts into Truckin’ and rolls straight into Black Peter. The crowd stayed rapt as the band closed with an upbeat Around & Around → Johnny B. Goode burst and a heartfelt It’s All Over Now Baby Blue encore. Across all recordings the performance is described as “totally locked in,” with Jerry “on fire,” Brent delivering standout keyboard moments, and Mickey driving dynamic rhythms even when Bill stepped offstage for a moment. The soundboard capture by Miller (gd1981-12-03.sbd.miller.108335.flac16) preserves every nuance – crystal‑clear mix, perfect patches, and just enough edge to let the band’s raw power shine.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All nine analyses carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire sequence, deep‑blues moments, and second‑set improvisations. No negative comments about performance quality appear, so a strong positive adjustment of +1.6 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crystal‑clear mix, perfect patches, and no drop‑outs; captures every highlight (Scarlet>Fire, Deep Elem Blues/Bertha, second‑set jams) with unmatched fidelity.

key_highlights: Monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence
Deep Elem Blues into a blistering Bertha
Space → Spanish Jam transition that rockets into Truckin'
Encore of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue
song_highlights: It's All Over Now Baby Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers blistering leads throughout, especially on Bertha, Big River, Deep Elem Blues and the Scarlet>Fire jam; reviewers describe him as "on fire" and his guitar work as "exquisite".
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid bass foundation; one reviewer notes his mix is slightly recessed but no performance criticism appears.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocals add energy and he contributes strong harmonies, though a few reviewers note a brief cut on Around & Around.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland supplies vivid keyboard textures, highlighted by a standout solo in the jam sections and tight harmony work on He’s Gone and the Spanish/O1 jam.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart drives dynamic drumming, especially during the Space‑to‑Spanish transition and post‑drum energy that never lets up; praised for his rhythmic leadership when Bill stepped offstage briefly.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann lays down a solid groove for most of the night, though one audience tape notes he left the stage momentarily, leaving Mickey to carry the drum section solo.

must_listen_sequences: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
